topics.title: Brain Dynamics and Individual Connectivity Variability
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain connectivity changes across states, tasks, and individuals, with emphasis on dynamic and functional network organization. It also highlights structure-function relationships and variability in brain dynamics across human and macaque studies.

topics.title: Alzheimer’s Disease Continuum and Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer’s disease across the preclinical to mild cognitive impairment stages, with emphasis on amyloid, tau, APOE, and cognitive decline. It also highlights MRI and white matter hyperintensities as biomarkers of progression and disease burden.

topics.title: Developmental Neurodevelopmental Risk and Outcomes
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain and cognitive development across childhood and adolescence, with attention to normative trajectories and individual differences. It also highlights how adversity, prematurity, and genetic or environmental factors shape neurodevelopmental risk and resilience.

topics.title: MRI Atlas and Segmentation Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-based atlas construction, cortical reconstruction, and automated segmentation approaches. It emphasizes machine learning and deep learning methods, including robustness and validation across high-resolution neuroimaging datasets.

topics.title: Depression and Related Psychiatric Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on depressive disorders across subtypes and ages, with attention to comorbid anxiety, obsessive-compulsive symptoms, bipolar depression, and suicidal ideation. It also includes treatment-resistant presentations and adolescent depressive outcomes.

topics.title: Neural Basis of Visual Imagery and Memory
topics.description: This cluster centers on how visual imagery, working memory, and recall are represented in the human brain, especially in visual cortex and hippocampus. It highlights neural correlates of encoding, attention, and episodic memory across vision-related tasks.

topics.title: Neural Bases of Emotion and Regulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how emotion regulation, emotion processing, and related neural correlates support affective and social functioning across anxiety, depression, fear, and resilience. It also includes studies of amygdala, interoception, empathy, and cognitive reappraisal in adolescence and clinical populations.

topics.title: Epilepsy Networks and Surgical Evaluation
topics.description: This cluster centers on epilepsy subtypes, presurgical evaluation, and the identification of epileptogenic zones using EEG, MEG, and intracranial recordings. It also highlights network-level brain changes and their relationships to therapy response and cognitive impairment.

topics.title: Brain Age and Normative Modeling in Aging
topics.description: This cluster focuses on brain age prediction and normative modeling to study aging-related differences in brain anatomy and cortical thickness across the adult lifespan. It also connects these measures to cognitive performance, cognitive reserve, and healthy aging.

topics.title: White Matter Microstructure Across Development
topics.description: Studies in this cluster examine white matter microstructure and structural organization using diffusion MRI and DTI, often focusing on age-related change, development, and adulthood. Several abstracts also relate these measures to traumatic brain injury and superficial or sulcal white matter.

topics.title: High-Resolution Mouse fMRI and Vascular Imaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on high-field, high-resolution mouse fMRI and related vascular imaging approaches, including protocol development, mesoscale imaging, and functional connectivity analysis. It emphasizes cerebrovascular reactivity, coupling, and fine-scale brain mapping in awake or anesthetized mice.

topics.title: Stroke Lesions and Motor-Aphasia Recovery
topics.description: This cluster centers on stroke and focal lesion effects on motor, language, and cognitive outcomes, with emphasis on aphasia, movement, and primary motor cortex involvement. It also includes imaging and stimulation studies examining structural disconnection and network reorganization after injury.

topics.title: Neural Basis of Language and Speech Processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on how language, speech, and comprehension are represented in the brain, with attention to multilingualism, development, and learning-related differences such as dyslexia. It also includes studies linking neural correlates to cognitive flexibility, music, and related behavioral measures.

topics.title: Genetic and Biomarker Heterogeneity in Psychosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on schizophrenia and related psychosis spectrum conditions, emphasizing transdiagnostic heterogeneity, early risk, and biomarker-based characterization. It also includes genetic and normative modeling approaches used to study cortical and other neuroanatomical differences.

topics.title: Neuromodulation for Psychiatric and Cognitive Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on noninvasive neuromodulation approaches, especially TMS, ITBS, TES, and focused ultrasound, and their efficacy across depression, cognitive impairment, and substance use disorders. It also emphasizes connectivity and resting-state fMRI as markers of target engagement and treatment response.

topics.title: Parkinsonian Neurodegeneration and Clinical Features
topics.description: This cluster centers on Parkinson disease and related neurodegenerative disorders, emphasizing pathology, brain regions, and symptoms such as freezing, hallucinations, and cognitive decline. It also includes deep brain stimulation and subthalamic nucleus-related studies within the broader disease context.

topics.title: Open Neuroimaging Workflow Standards
topics.description: This cluster centers on open, standardized neuroimaging workflows for organizing, converting, processing, and sharing brain imaging data. It emphasizes BIDS-based frameworks, automation, and software tools that support reproducible and clinically actionable analyses.

keywords: autism sensory subtypes predict task evoked insula connectivity
abnormal functional hierarchy development during childhood in autism
topics.title: Autism Subtypes and Brain Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on autism spectrum disorder, including symptom profiles, subgroups, and developmental differences across autistic individuals and youth. It also highlights brain abnormalities and connectivity-related findings linked to functional development in autism.

topics.title: MRI Biomarkers of Fatigue and Brain Change
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-based characterization of brain microstructure, iron, and connectivity changes associated with fatigue and neurological disease, especially multiple sclerosis and related conditions. It also includes studies of age-related and structural abnormalities using quantitative and advanced imaging approaches.

topics.title: Aperiodic and Alpha Neural Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on EEG/MEG markers of brain state, especially alpha oscillations and aperiodic activity, and their links to consciousness, attention, and cognitive state changes. It also includes broader measures of cortical dynamics and neural signal complexity.

keywords: eeg resting state complexity and entropy
topics.title: Sleep, Arousal, and Brain State Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how sleep stages, arousal transitions, and consciousness relate to dynamic brain activity and functional connectivity. It also includes work on EEG complexity, aging effects, and the comparison of sleep with anesthesia depth.

keywords: functional connectivity pathways support fear avoidance processes
topics.title: Brain mechanisms of chronic pain
topics.description: This cluster centers on neural mechanisms and brain markers associated with chronic pain and pain perception across human studies. It also includes network-level pathways, central sensitization, and condition-specific pain phenotypes such as trigeminal neuralgia.

keywords: functional connectivity predict crave across substance use disorder
topics.title: Craving and Neural Predictors in Addiction
topics.description: This cluster focuses on addiction-related craving, abstinence, and substance use disorders, with emphasis on neural predictors and reward circuitry across alcohol, methamphetamine, opioid, and cannabis use. It also includes studies of functional and effective connectivity as markers of craving and related behavior.

keywords: gradient organization of cortical metabolic connectivity
topics.title: Human Brain Metabolic Network Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how glucose metabolism and other metabolic processes shape human brain network organization and functional dynamics. It links exercise, insulin, and hyperglycemia to metabolic signatures and connectomic changes measured with spectroscopy and related imaging methods.

keywords: inclusive unsedated brain mri protocol for neurodiverse children with behaviours of concern
topics.title: Motion-Reduced MRI and Brain Function Studies
topics.description: This cluster centers on strategies to reduce head motion and improve MRI feasibility in children and other challenging populations, including unsedated protocols, training approaches, and virtual reality support. It also includes related neuroscience studies of cognition, sex differences, mindfulness, and brain network function.

keywords: the motion sick brain spectral changes
topics.title: Neural Changes in Motion Sickness and Recovery
topics.description: Studies of the motion sick brain emphasize spectral changes, structural network reorganization, and related metabolic patterns during vestibular challenge. Research often uses rotating chair experiments, electroencephalography, and PET-based approaches to track vestibular recovery.

topics.title: Human Circumventricular Organ Architecture
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the structural organization of the human circumventricular organ. It appears to center on anatomical architecture rather than a specific experimental technique.

topics.title: Microglial and Transcriptomic Imaging Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster focuses on noninvasive approaches for studying microglial status and related molecular changes in the brain. It emphasizes diffusion-weighted MRS and transcriptomic signals as biomarkers for neuroscience research.

topics.title: Hypersexuality in Neurological Disorders
topics.description: This cluster focuses on hypersexuality as it appears in neurological disorders. The abstracts appear to synthesize evidence through systematic review.
